headline: Newspaper licence denials affect 5 independent outlets
context: The Home Ministry declined to renew printing licences for 5 independent news outlets in 2024-2025 under the Printing Presses and Publications Act.

cards.big: 5 independent news outlets lost printing licences in 2024-2025
cards.sub: Home Minister's discretion under PPPA requires no justification for denial

cards.big: PPPA Section 6 gives Home Minister absolute power to revoke licences
cards.sub: Courts cannot review the merits of licence denial decisions

cards.big: Article 10(1)(a) guarantees freedom of speech including press freedom
cards.sub: Licensing regime creates prior restraint incompatible with free press principles

cards.big: 3 of 5 outlets had published investigations into government-linked companies
cards.sub: Pattern suggests editorial independence correlates with licence risk

cards.big: Combined readership of 5 outlets exceeded 2.3 million monthly readers
cards.sub: Licence denial effectively silences journalism that serves public interest

cards.big: When 5 watchdog outlets lose licences, the public loses its eyes
Press licensing that requires no justification is indistinguishable from censorship. The question is whether Malaysia wants accountability or silence.
